title: The Death of Syntax: Why “Coding” is Becoming a Product Engineering Role in 2026
author: Kapil Khatik
excerpt:
The Death of Syntax: Why “Coding” is Becoming a Product Engineering Role in 2026
The “Junior Developer” role as we knew it — the person who translates a ticket into a function, wrestles with semicolon placement, and memorizes standard library APIs — is dead.
It hasn’t been killed by a lack of work. It has been sublimated.
With the maturity of agentic IDEs like Cursor and Windsurf, and workflow automators like GitHub Copilot Workspace, the barrier to entry for writing code has collapsed. But the
